items.title: Pick the norm to match the problem geometry
items.avoid: Defaulting to L2 for every task without considering feature scale, sparsity or whether direction matters more than magnitude.
items.prefer: Use L2 on scaled continuous features, cosine on embeddings, L1 when sparse outliers or grid-like structure matter.
items.why: The norm defines nearness; mismatched geometry yields poor neighbors, clusters and retrieval results.
items.example: For 768-d sentence embeddings in a RAG pipeline, cosine similarity in the vector DB—not raw L2 on unnormalized vectors—is the usual choice.

items.title: Validate distance behavior on toy data
items.avoid: Deploying a distance-based model without inspecting a few nearest-neighbor examples manually.
items.prefer: Print the k nearest points for several queries and verify they are semantically sensible after preprocessing.
items.why: Silent preprocessing bugs (wrong axis, forgotten scaling) show up immediately in neighbor lists.
items.example: After building a k-NN classifier, query a known class centroid and confirm the top neighbors share the label before evaluating accuracy.
